🌅 Morning Briefing

Saturday, March 21, 2026

🌍 Global News
Trump says US considering 'winding down' Iran war as Natanz nuclear facility attacked again
President Trump announced the US may wind down military operations against Iran as the war enters its fourth week. Iranian media reported the Natanz nuclear enrichment facility was struck again. Over 2,000 people have been killed in Iran since attacks began on February 28.
Reuters / AP / Al Jazeera · War/Middle East
Iran targets Diego Garcia military base in Indian Ocean, demonstrating expanded missile range
Iran attacked the joint UK-US military base on Diego Garcia, approximately 2,500 miles away, suggesting Tehran possesses missiles with greater range than previously acknowledged or used its space program for an improvised launch.
AP News · War/Middle East
Iran's naval forces claim attacks on UAE and Kuwait military facilities
Iran says its naval forces destroyed facilities at the Al-Minhad base in the UAE and the Ali Al Salem airbase in Kuwait, claiming they were used in attacks on Iranian territory.
Al Jazeera · War/Middle East
Trump calls NATO 'cowardly' over lack of support for Iran war
Trump criticized NATO allies for not supporting the Iran war effort and stated that other nations who use the Strait of Hormuz should take the lead in protecting it, not the United States.
Al Jazeera · Politics/Diplomacy
Oil prices up 50% due to Iran war, hobbling global economy
The war's effects are being felt far beyond the Middle East, raising food and fuel prices globally. The near-closure of the Strait of Hormuz threatens a global energy shock.
Reuters · Economy/Energy
🤖 AI News
Google expands personalized Gemini AI to all US users
Google is rolling out its Personal Intelligence feature to all US users, allowing Gemini to draw on data from connected apps like Gmail, Photos, and YouTube to deliver more context-aware responses.
MarketingProfs · product
White House releases AI policy blueprint for Congress
The White House published a long-awaited policy wishlist for federal regulation of artificial intelligence, hoping Congress will codify it into law.
Politico · policy
Anthropic announces 1M token context window for Claude
Anthropic announced general availability of 1 million token context windows for Claude Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 at standard pricing, with no beta header and media cap up to 600.
Releasebot · product
AI startups accounted for 41% of venture capital in 2025
AI startups accounted for 41% of the $128 billion in venture dollars raised by companies on Carta last year — a record-high annual share. VCs have invested over half a trillion dollars into AI over the last five years.
TechCrunch · funding
Jeff Bezos reportedly seeking $100B for AI-focused industrial fund
Jeff Bezos is reportedly seeking to raise $100 billion for an AI-focused industrial investment fund, which would be one of the largest investment vehicles ever created.
Business Insider · funding
📰 Seattle News
🌤️ Partly Cloudy
49° / 38°
Partly sunny with slight chance of rain
UW Cherry Blossoms Reach Peak Bloom
The University of Washington's iconic cherry blossom trees reached peak bloom on March 20, 2026. The 29 Yoshino cherry trees on the UW Quad are now in full display, marking the arrival of spring in Seattle.
University of Washington / KUOW · lifestyle
Landslide Blocks Northbound I-5 Near Bellingham
A landslide blocked all northbound lanes of I-5 near Bellingham late Thursday night (March 19), closing the highway just north of North Lake Samish Drive. Approximately 3,000 cubic yards of debris covered the roadway.
FOX 13 Seattle / Bellingham Herald · traffic
Blazing Bagels Closing All Locations Amid Bankruptcy
Beloved Seattle bagel chain Blazing Bagels is closing all locations after filing for Chapter 7 bankruptcy. The company, which operated five locations across the Seattle area, cited rising costs and decreased foot traffic.
Seattle Times / KING 5 · business
West Seattle Recycle/Reuse/Shred Event Today
The West Seattle Junction Association is hosting a free recycling and shredding event today from 9 AM to 1 PM at the Junction Plaza Park. Residents can bring documents for shredding and items for recycling.
West Seattle Blog · community
